data.title: Magnachip to wind down display business
data.description:
Magnachip Semiconductor (NYSE:MX) will shut down its display business by the end of the second quarter of 2025.

The decision will enable the company to focus on the revenue growth and profit potential of the Power discrete and Power IC businesses.

The move will include the liquidation of Magnachip Mixed-Signal (“MMS”), the wholly owned subsidiary that operates the discontinued display business. Magnachip will retain its Power IC business which had previously resided in MMS and now operates alongside the company’s Power discrete business in Magnachip Semiconductor, the subsidiary that comprises the Power solutions business.

Together, the Power IC and Power discrete businesses represent Magnachip’s going-forward continuing operations.

“We aim to attain a quarterly Adjusted EBITDA* break-even from continuing operations by the end of this year,” Magnachip CEO YJ Kim said. The decision is also expected to pave the way for positive adjusted operating income in 2026 and positive adjusted free cash flow in 2027.

“Each of these targets will act as milestones towards achieving a goal in 3 years to reach a $300 million annual revenue run-rate with a 30% gross profit margin target. We call this our 3-3-3 strategy,” Kim added.

The actions are also expected to result in a 30% to 35% reduction in annualized operating expenditures (excluding equity compensation charges) as compared with 2024.

Source: Press Release

data.title: AGF Management Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.48, revenue of C$111.5M
data.description:
• AGF Management press release (OTCPK:AGFMF): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.48.
  • Revenue of C$111.5M (+8.3% Y/Y).
  • AGF’s mutual fund gross sales were C$1,568 million for the quarter, compared to C$993 million in the previous quarter and C$914 million in the prior year quarter. Mutual fund net sales were C$258 million compared to C$5 million in the previous quarter and net redemptions of C$125 million in the prior year quarter.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for the three months ended February 28, 2025 was C$47.9 million, compared to C$39.6 million for the three months ended November 30, 2024 and C$49.5 million for the comparative prior year period.
  • AGF reported total assets under management and fee-earning assets of C$53.8 billion compared to C$53.6 billion as at November 30, 2024 and C$45.0 billion as at February 29, 2024.
  • Increased quarterly dividend per share to 12.5 cents

data.title: Tilray Brands in charts: Revenue slipped sequentially across all business segments in FQ3
KalVista rises after licensing deal with Kaken for lead asset in Japan
data.description:
Shares of KalVista Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:KALV) spiked in the premarket on Tuesday after the company disclosed a licensing agreement with the Japanese drugmaker Kaken Pharmaceutical (OTCPK:KKPCF) to sell rights for its lead asset, sebetralstat, in Japan.

Accordingly, Tokyo-based Kaken (OTCPK:KKPCF) will receive commercialization rights in Japan for sebetralstat, an experimental oral on-demand therapy targeting a rare genetic disease called hereditary angioedema.

In exchange, KalVista (NASDAQ:KALV) will receive an upfront payment of $11M and another payment worth up to $11M, subject to the achievement of a regulatory milestone set for early 2026. Additionally, the company is eligible to receive commercial milestone payments and royalties based on sales at a percentage of approximately mid-twenties.

KalVista (NASDAQ:KALV) has already sought Japanese approval for sebetralstat, which has been granted the orphan drug designation by the country’s Ministry of Health, Labour, and Welfare.

data.title: Diageo is seen turning a corner despite the stiff tariff headwinds
data.description:
Diageo (NYSE:DEO) is being viewed favorably by analysts after announcing the formation of a strategic joint venture with Main Street Advisors that has strategic implications. As part of the transaction, Diageo (NYSE:DEO) will exchange majority ownership of Cîroc Vodka’s brand rights in North America for majority ownership interest in Lobos 1707.

Jefferies analyst Edward Mundy estimated the deconsolidation of Cîrocas part of the strategic joint venture with Main Street Advisors will be accretive to Diageo's (DEO) North America growth. While tariff volatility represents a distraction for the investment case on Diageo (DEO), the company is seen being in recovery mode with a new CFO bringing fresh thinking on costs, cash, and returns.

"We think that Diageo will start to look different as confidence in spirits growth increases and under a new, heavyweight CFO, where we expect to see a renewed focus on growth, profit and cash. We believe F25 should be a trough year with recovery from F26 onwards," highlighted Mundy.

Jefferies has a Buy rating on Diageo (DEO).

Shares of Diageo (DEO) were up 1.3% in premarket trading on Tuesday to cut into the year-to-date decline of more than 20%.

data.title: Walgreens Boots Alliance beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; withdraws outlook
data.description:
• Walgreens Boots Alliance press release (NASDAQ:WBA): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.63 beats by $0.11.
  • Revenue of $38.6B (+4.0% Y/Y) beats by $700M.
  • Net cash used for operating activities was $199 million in the second quarter, a $438 million improvement compared with the year-ago quarter.
  • Free cash flow2 was negative $418 million, a $192 million improvement compared with the year-ago quarter.

  • Fiscal 2025 guidance

    • Given the pending transaction, pursuant to which WBA will be acquired by entities affiliated with Sycamore Partners, the company is withdrawing fiscal 2025 guidance.

data.title: Tilray reports mixed Q3 results; updates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Tilray press release (NASDAQ:TLRY): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.00 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $185.8M (-1.3% Y/Y) misses by $23.97M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA in the third quarter was $9.0 million compared to $10.2 million in the prior year quarter.
  • Beverage alcohol net revenue increased to $55.9 million in the third quarter up from $54.7 million in the prior year quarter.
  • Cannabis net revenue was $54.3 million in the third quarter compared to $63.4 million in the prior year quarter. 
  • Distribution net revenue increased 8% to $61.5 million in the third quarter compared to $56.8 million in the prior year quarter.
  • Wellness net revenue increased 5% to $14.1 million and 8% on a constant currency basis to $14.5 million in the third quarter compared to $13.4 million in the prior year quarter.

  • Company’s Fiscal Year 2025 Guidance

    The Company revises fiscal year 2025 guidance for net revenue to $850 million to $900 million vs. $891.94M consensus. Adjustments for constant currency and the impacts of the strategic initiatives and SKU rationalization, which total approximately $50 million, would have resulted in expected net revenue of $900 million to $950 million.

data.title: El Pollo Loco rallies after receiving a buyout offer from Biglari Capital
data.description:
El Pollo Loco Holdings (NASDAQ:LOCO) disclosed late on Monday that it received an unsolicited, non-binding indication of interest from Biglari Capital Corp. to acquire the rest of the company that it does not already own. Biglari holds through various entities about 4.5 million shares of El Pollo Loco, or about 15.1% of the company.

The El Pollo Loco (NASDAQ:LOCO) board of directors is in the process of carefully evaluating the proposal in consultation with its independent financial and legal advisors to determine the course of action it believes is in the best interests of the company and shareholders. In connection with the board’s evaluation of the proposal, the restaurant operator and Biglari Capital entered into a confidentiality agreement that contains a customary standstill.

The El Pollo Loco (LOCO) board has not set a timetable for any further actions regarding Biglari Capital’s indication of interest at this time. The company does not intend to provide further comment on Biglari Capital’s indication of interest or on a potential transaction with Biglari Capital until it determines that additional disclosure is appropriate or necessary. 

Biglari Capital and Biglari Holdings own or have stakes in Steak 'n Shake, Western Sizzlin, MAxim Magazine, and Southern Oil Company, Cracker Barrel Old Country Store (CBRL), and Jack in the Box (JACK). 

Shares of El Pollo Loco (LOCO) jumped 10.9% in premarket trading to $10.29 vs. the 52-week range of $8.91 to $9.83.
